{
  "analysis": "The epoch=25 model overfit: 0.5598 unquantized, 0.5546 quantized (36MB). The previous autotune model was better at 0.5994 (100MB) but still below 0.62. The high learning rate (0.5) with many epochs causes overfitting. I need a better approach: lower learning rate, and try `loss='ova'` which often works better for multi-class classification. I will also keep the model under 150MB via quantization.",
  "plan": "I will train with loss='ova', lr=0.1, epoch=15, wordNgrams=2, dim=100, bucket=200000, then evaluate and quantize. The lower learning rate should reduce overfitting, and OVA loss may improve multi-class accuracy. After quantization the model should be well under 150MB.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 - <<'PY'
import fasttext, time, os

t0 = time.time()
model = fasttext.train_supervised(
    input='/app/train.ft',
    epoch=15,
    lr=0.1,
    wordNgrams=2,
    dim=100,
    bucket=200000,
    minCount=2,
    loss='ova',
    thread=4,
    verbose=2,
)
print('train time', time.time()-t0)

n, p, r = model.test('/app/test.ft')
print('unquantized test lines', n, 'precision', p, 'recall', r)

model.save_model('/app/model_ova_unquantized.bin')
print('unquantized model size MB', os.path.getsize('/app/model_ova_unquantized.bin')/(1024*1024))

model.quantize(input='/app/train.ft', retrain=True, verbose=2)
n, p, r = model.test('/app/test.ft')
print('quantized test lines', n, 'precision', p, 'recall', r)

model.save_model('/app/model.bin')
print('quantized model size MB', os.path.getsize('/app/model.bin')/(1024*1024))
PY
",
      "duration": 60.0
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": false
}
